2606.14654.title: Abstracting Cross-Domain Action Sequences into Interpretable Workflows
2606.14654.paper_summary: Sequential or time-stamped interaction logs provide objective records of digital application usage, yet their granularity and noise often obscure meaningful insights into people's work. Such insights are essential for improving digital products in ways grounded in real-world user interactions. Prior research has applied deep learning models to cluster user actions into high-level activities, but these approaches are highly sensitive to noise and struggle to generalize across applications. To address this limitation, we introduce WorkflowView, a framework that uses large language models (LLMs) to abstract low-level action sequences into high-level activities. We establish the effectiveness and generality of our approach across three distinct, challenging sequential tasks and diverse domains: (a) zero-shot task description reconstruction from browser logs (achieving high semantic similarity, $μ_{sim} = 0.91$), (b) few-shot student dropout prediction using MOOC interaction logs (reaching weighted $F_1 = 0.90$ with only five few-shot examples), and (c) anonymized, privacy-preserving analysis of AI tool integration within document workflows in Microsoft Word. Our work demonstrates that LLM-based abstraction is a robust and efficient path forward for transforming low-level behavioral data into high-level, interpretable, and actionable insights. We also discuss practical considerations for deploying LLM-based inferences within logging infrastructures, including computational efficiency and user privacy.
2606.14654.paper_summary_zh: 順序或時間戳互動日誌提供了數位應用程式使用的客觀記錄，然而它們的細節和噪音常常掩蓋了對人們工作有意義的洞察。這些洞察對於以真實用戶互動為基礎的方式改善數位產品至關重要。先前的研究已經應用深度學習模型將用戶行為聚類為高層次活動，但這些方法對噪音高度敏感，並且在不同應用之間的泛化能力不足。為了解決這一限制，我們引入了WorkflowView，一個使用大型語言模型（LLMs）將低層次行動序列抽象為高層次活動的框架。我們在三個不同且具挑戰性的順序任務和多樣化領域中建立了我們方法的有效性和普遍性：（a）從瀏覽器日誌中進行零樣本任務描述重建（實現高語義相似度，$μ_{sim} = 0.91$），（b）使用MOOC互動日誌進行少樣本學生輟學預測（僅用五個少樣本範例達到加權$F_1 = 0.90$），以及（c）在Microsoft Word文件工作流程中對AI工具整合的匿名、隱私保護分析。我們的工作展示了基於LLM的抽象是一條穩健且高效的道路，能將低層次行為數據轉換為高層次、可解釋且可行的洞察。我們還討論了在日誌基礎設施中部署基於LLM的推論的實際考量，包括計算效率和用戶隱私。
2606.14654.author: Gaurav Verma et.al.
